title: delegation — published events
description: Ten events split across two aggregates. All fire `afterCommit` so downstream listeners never see uncommitted state. Payloads are readonly value objects (readonly class + readonly properties) — immutable by construction. Impersonation events with SLA implications route to the `notifications-critical` queue so they never queue behind a backlog (SOC 2 evidence writes cannot be delayed). Every consumer listed is a listener declared in listeners.json — the pairing is the traceability contract.

events.name: DelegationCreated
events.trigger: RoleDelegationObserver::created hook after the row is inserted + all validators passed. Fires exactly once per delegation, ever.

payload.actor_id: ULID (the caller — always the delegator on POST /me/delegations)

events.name: DelegationActivated
events.trigger: ActivateDelegationsJob observed `starts_at <= now() AND state was pending`. Also fires when a delegation is created with starts_at already in the past (i.e. immediate activation on POST). Delegate gets notified — 'you can now act as X'.

events.name: DelegationExpired
events.trigger: ExpireDelegationsJob observed `ends_at <= now() AND revoked_at IS NULL AND state was active`. Terminal state. Delegator gets notified.

events.name: DelegationRevokedEarly
events.trigger: DELETE /me/delegations/{delegation} succeeded — the delegator (or admin) revoked before ends_at was reached. RoleDelegationObserver::updated on `revoked_at` transitioning from null to non-null.

payload.revoked_by: ULID (usr_) — the delegator OR an admin
payload.planned_ends_at: ISO-8601 timestamp (the original ends_at, retained for audit narrative)

events.name: DelegationSelfOverlapDetected
events.trigger: RoleDelegationObserver::creating rejected a POST because the delegator already has an ACTIVE delegation of the same role to the same delegate with a window that overlaps the requested one. Critical severity — signals a UX bug in the tenant admin surface OR an abuse attempt. No row is created; the event fires so security + product can investigate.

payload.existing_delegation_id: ULID (dlg_) — the overlapping row that caused refusal

events.name: ImpersonationSessionStarted
events.trigger: ImpersonationSessionObserver::created after all safety gates passed (entitlement check, PlatformUser is active, target tenant is enabled, snapshot fields captured). CRITICAL severity — routes to notifications-critical + fires the cannot-opt-out notification to the impersonated User.

events.name: ImpersonationSessionEnded
events.trigger: ImpersonationSessionObserver::updated on ended_at transitioning from null to non-null. Fires for end_trigger IN (self_service, ttl_expired). NOT for admin_revocation / security_lockdown — those fire the dedicated ForcedTerminate event instead.

events.name: ImpersonationSessionExtended
events.trigger: A super_admin extended the session's ends_at (rare — the tenant must hold `impersonation_extended_ttl` entitlement + only super_admin can invoke). ImpersonationSessionObserver::updated on ends_at increase.

events.name: ImpersonationSessionForcedTerminate
events.trigger: Security team fired `POST /impersonation/{session}/force-terminate` — a lockdown event. Sets ended_at + end_trigger=security_lockdown. Notifies security team via ImpersonationForcedTerminationNotification (cannot-opt-out).

events.name: ImpersonationAttemptRefused
events.trigger: ImpersonationSessionObserver::creating refused. Reason falls into: entitlement missing (PlatformUser lacks `platform.impersonate`), PlatformUser status != active, target User doesn't exist / belongs to different tenant, target tenant is disabled, target application is disabled, TTL request out of bounds, reason too short. No row created; the event fires so security can spot patterns.

payload.refusal_reason: string — machine-readable code (missing_entitlement / operator_not_active / target_not_found / cross_tenant_target / target_tenant_disabled / target_application_disabled / ttl_out_of_bounds / reason_too_short)
